assessment.summary_for_seller: Good run: you held context, answered on-point, and brought the conversation to a next step.

criteria.name: Contextual first contact
criteria.short_reason: First message anchors to a specific signal from the card.
criteria.evidence_quote: Irina, BlueMesa has 30+ contractors and payout coordination is spread across chats and spreadsheets. Mellow gives you a predictable process: auto-payments, real-time statuses, no manual follow-ups. Concretely — what takes the most ops time per week right now?
criteria.label: Contextual opening

criteria.name: Compliance and boundary of responsibility
criteria.short_reason: No clear boundary of responsibility or specifics on Mellow's scope.
criteria.evidence_quote: Pricing model: per contractor/month, with volume tiers. There's a pilot option to test on a subset before committing. Want me to send a rough calculation?
criteria.label: Scope & responsibility boundary

criteria.name: Language fit
criteria.short_reason: Language is adapted to the chosen persona.
criteria.evidence_quote: Irina, BlueMesa has 30+ contractors and payout coordination is spread across chats and spreadsheets. Mellow gives you a predictable process: auto-payments, real-time statuses, no manual follow-ups. Concretely — what takes the most ops time per week right now?
criteria.label: Persona language fit

criteria.name: Handling core objections
criteria.short_reason: At least two key objections addressed substantively.
criteria.evidence_quote: Irina, BlueMesa has 30+ contractors and payout coordination is spread across chats and spreadsheets. Mellow gives you a predictable process: auto-payments, real-time statuses, no manual follow-ups. Concretely — what takes the most ops time per week right now?

objections.objection: You spotted the pain. But concretely: what stops happening manually, and how much time does that recover?
Wait. I still don't understand how to bring this to finance.
Send me one screen with the flow and what specifically stops being manual. If it's real, we can talk.
Send it. If it's concrete — I'll respond.

criteria.label: Core objection handling

criteria.name: Next step
criteria.evidence_quote: Irina, BlueMesa has 30+ contractors and payout coordination is spread across chats and spreadsheets. Mellow gives you a predictable process: auto-payments, real-time statuses, no manual follow-ups. Concretely — what takes the most ops time per week right now?
criteria.label: Clear next step

transcript.text: Irina, BlueMesa has 30+ contractors and payout coordination is spread across chats and spreadsheets. Mellow gives you a predictable process: auto-payments, real-time statuses, no manual follow-ups. Concretely — what takes the most ops time per week right now?
You spotted the pain. But concretely: what stops happening manually, and how much time does that recover?
Pricing model: per contractor/month, with volume tiers. There's a pilot option to test on a subset before committing. Want me to send a rough calculation?
Wait. I still don't understand how to bring this to finance.
Pricing model: per contractor/month, with volume tiers. There's a pilot option to test on a subset before committing. Want me to send a rough calculation?
Send me one screen with the flow and what specifically stops being manual. If it's real, we can talk.
